title: narcissus × brevitubulosus subsp. alejandrei
intro: Narcissus × brevitubulosus subsp. alejandrei, also known as the Alejandrino narcissus, is a hybrid plant species within the Amaryllidaceae family, specifically a cross between N. brevitubulosus and an unidentified species of narcissus. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the acetylcholinesterase enzyme, which is associated with the treatment of Alzheimer's disease and other neurodegenerative disorders. The health benefits of Narcissus × brevitubulosus subsp. alejandrei include its potential to improve cognitive function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a subject of interest in herbal medicine. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared in various herbal preparations, such as tinctures, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues.
